Or they come or they all got a story they all come over and my cousin had one of those and you know It's a big deal. That's the fun of it for everybody had one. That'd be no fun Right, so I get to give them something rare. I'll take a few clients. Oh, right Bragging rights is attached to that Right. They get to go tell everybody They got me working on their stuff. Sometimes that even has commercial use for them Curiosity appeased that's a value. I give them. There's a lot of curiosity When I did my original mastermind groups, we had those in the basement too 18 people and they that was a big stuff. They love coming to the basement snooping around Right. That was a big deal trash. They love trash You should remember that by the way if you ever if you ever have groups in your house, they love your trash Because that's a big curiosity thing, you know just like When you go visit somebody look at in a medicine cabinet, right? You guys all everybody does it You all look some people take shit, but most Mostly mostly everybody just looks it's so common remember Southwest Airlines used it in a commercial Right the person snooping in a medicine cabinet and the shelf collapses and all the crap falls on the floor And there's stuff everywhere and yet you want to get away, right? So this is so common Southwest Airlines used it in a commercial if it's not your behavior It's everybody else's behavior and you should know everybody that comes to your house is looking in your medicine cabinet So, you know, you don't want to leave anything in there that's embarrassing and obvious because they're go snoop, right? That's what they're going to do. So they love going through my trash So I if I was ever having a mastermind meeting I let trash accumulate for a while. Well, you don't want to take it out They're unhappy. All right, they like getting in there and finding shit and taking it with them It's a big deal, you know And so that's a value, right also saves me a little money on having somebody haul out to trash But that but that's a value that it's not does it add it I don't know if it adds anything really to their master my experience or not reassurance insurance Superstition that's a value that I deliver my entire Relationship with a client I had for a very long time Guthi Rinker was originated by and largely sustained by pure superstition And I was well aware of it So the first thing I worked on Was dying and I saved it The second thing they did was personal power. I barely had anything to do with it But a little big hit now, it's like having a Buddha doll you rub his tummy, you know before you do anything They don't want to do anything without me around right because maybe that was it They don't know they know they've done two shows. They've had two hits and one of the commonalities is I was standing around Well, so I'm getting paid to stand around for a while. Right. It was pure superstition for a lot of people It's reassurance. It's not that they couldn't say go do it on their own But they feel better spending a hundred or two hundred grand on a rollout knowing I Did something so there's buying reassurance or they feel like they're buying insurance encouragement for some that's encouragement they're buying somebody to Push them to get something done to get something out into the marketplace All right, so they're buying that for some they're buying cocktail party stories now the important thing is most of that adds nothing to the intrinsic Value of what's delivered to them. It only is the other Kind of value, but the other kind of value is where we have all the room to maneuver big thing in your head The big thing to get okay with is this That one kind of value is just as legitimate as the other So in a conversation I just had for the last half hour I Distinguished between the two I differentiated between the two But the big thing to get is that one's just as valuable as the other so the getting of the cocktail party story By having gone to Disney instead of Six Flags is The same it's not a lesser class of value Than getting great rides It's the same and we tend to mentally discriminate against it and feel Less by delivering a lot of one kind of value versus another
